title: Vintage Curry takes over as Dubs extend season
content: The Warriors, led by Stephen Curry, erased a 13-point deficit in the fourth quarter to edge the Clippers and advance in the NBA's play-in tournament.

title: Minus Embiid, Maxey propels Sixers into playoffs
content: With Joel Embiid watching from the bench, Tyrese Maxey scored 31 points to lead the Sixers past the Magic 109-97 and lock up a first-round playoff date with the rival Boston Celtics.

title: Hornets' Ball fined $35K for 'reckless' Bam play
content: The NBA has fined LaMelo Ball $35,000 "for making unnecessary and reckless contact" with Bam Adebayo during Tuesday's play-in game. The Hornets star also was fined $25,000 for using profane language during a TV interview after that game.

title: Kawhi mum on future, 'not stressing' NBA probe
content: Kawhi Leonard declined to discuss his future with the Clippers after Wednesday's play-in loss and said he's "not stressing" the NBA's investigation into his deal with Aspiration.

title: LIV CEO says season to continue 'at full throttle'
content: Amid reports that Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund might be on the verge of pulling its funding for the league, LIV Golf CEO Scott O'Neil told staff in an email Wednesday that its season will go on "as planned, uninterrupted and at full throttle."

title: Sources: MLBPA fires two top execs with cause
content: The Major League Baseball Players Association fired two top executives with cause Wednesday following an internal investigation, sources told ESPN.

title: Ulberg says he lost UFC title belt celebrating win
content: Newly crowned UFC light heavyweight champion Carlos Ulberg told Fox Sports Australia that he misplaced his title belt while celebrating his victory.

title: Ohtani skips bat, cranks up pitches in 10-K gem
content: Not in the lineup for his start day Wednesday, Shohei Ohtani channeled all his energy into pitching and delivered a six-inning, 10-strikeout start Wednesday night in an 8-2 win over the Mets.

title: How is a constitution amendment Bill passed?
content: A Constitutional Amendment Bill must secure two-thirds of majority votes in both Houses, and get ratified by at least 50% State legislatures, if it affects the federal structure, before receiving an assent from President.

title: Delimitation Bill will not affect Tamil Nadu: AIADMK leader Edappadi Palaniswami
content: When Union Minister Amit Shah visited Tamil Nadu, he assured that the State will not be affected, stated Mr. Palaniswami. “The NDA will implement delimitation better than what Mr. Stalin expects. The Centre is clear that no State will be affected by the delimitation,” he added.

title: Ahead of Assembly election results, Congress leadership debate in Kerala heats up

While Ramesh Chennithala says party high command will take final call, KPCC president Sunny Joseph points out ‘senior leaders should not jump the gun’ on the matter. Left supporters seek to characterise ‘feuding’ in Congress as a move aimed at testing ‘factional loyalties and flushing out potential rivals’

content: Broadly, the four volumes has data on percentage of people — across social categories — who benefitted from reservations in various categories of education and government employment; livelihoods, education levels and other key aspects.

title: Explained | Assam’s delimitation test
content: What is the Election Commission’s draft proposal on delimitation? Why are people opposing it? Who is going to get affected by the exercise? Why do some parties want the delimitation done after the National Register of Citizens is updated? Is there a possibility of the proposal being redrafted?

title: Assam delimitation proposal: an erosion of representation
content: The proposed exercise is allegedly based on communal lines of segregation, rather than on logical geographic boundaries of the State. The plan, which also involves reservations, may reduce the possibility of Muslims being elected, diminishing the existing political power of the minority community

title: Delimitation saga: Wards reduced to 225 ahead of BBMP polls
content: Repeated demands to publish digital interactive maps of the proposed wards and to allow objections to be filed online have fallen on deaf ears

content: The delimitation exercise, constitutionally mandated for 2026, looms large over the debate, and the government and the BJP may have to evolve a response sooner rather than later

content: The newly elected MPs represent Assam, Bihar, Haryana, Chhattisgarh, Himachal Pradesh, Telangana, Odisha and Maharashtra.

content: Day after factory workers’ protests turn violent in Noida, 350 arrested; police conduct flag marches; additional forces deployed across industrial area; workers continue to demand improved working conditions; police use lathi charge, tear gas to disperse domestic workers outside high-rise society

title: How Tamil Nadu got 234 Assembly seats: The Delimitation Commission’s calculated departure
content: Under Article 170 of the Constitution and Section 8(b) of the Delimitation Commission Act, 1962, the number of seats in a State Assembly must be an integral multiple of the number of Lok Sabha seats allotted to that State

title: When welfare met demographic concerns
content: A new study revisits the parliamentary debates in the 1960s over linking maternity benefits to population control in India

content: With Himanta Biswa Sarma criss-crossing the State as campaigner-in-chief, the challenge for local BJP canvassers in Jalukbari is to ensure healthy numbers in order to match or better the CM’s six-figure margin from 2021

content: The drones, with a payload capacity of 20 kg, can carry water, extinguishing powder or firefighting equipment, and will be used in emergencies

title: Araku MP Thanuja Rani seeks reopening of Chinabhogili railway gate in Parvatipuram-Manyam
content: Araku Member of Parliament Gumma Thanuja Rani urges the railway authorities to reopen the Chinabhogili railway gate, the only route of travel for nearly 10,000 people across five villages of Parvatipuram-Manyam district

content: Chief Minister M.K Stalin has convened an all-party meeting on March 5 to discuss the impact of the proposed delimitation exercise.

content: The Prime Minister said India and Austria are in agreement that military conflict cannot resolve problems

content: The plan to link women’s reservation in Parliament with delimitation has created a sharp political divide.

content: Thulya Prathinidhya Prasthanam raises five demands ahead of Assembly polls, calls for gender-balanced politics in Kerala.

content: An Indian amla tree, centuries ago, lent its name to one of the world's most important waterways, the Strait of Malacca

content: The pope said last week those who believe in Christ  is never on the side of those who "once wielded the sword and today drop bombs."

content: Individuals whose work, study or tourist visas are nearing expiry in the UK are being guided to claim they face danger because of their sexual orientation.

content: Rights groups accuse Iran of using the death penalty as a tool of repression to instil fear among the prople amid war against Israel and the US.

title: Retire Rich By 45? Step-By-Step Guide To Building A Rs 3 Crore Corpus
content: A retirement corpus is not a random financial target. It is a replacement plan for your income when your salary stops.

title: At Private Dinner With Dutch Royals, Trump Reveals His Plan To End Iran War
content: The royal meal laid bare how alone Trump stands in his war with Iran, even though he repeatedly declares victory while declaring the conflict is "very close to over".

title: 'Bindi, Tilak Not Restricted': Lenskart Founder Clarifies After Row Over Grooming Policy
content: Bansal clarified that the company has revised the grooming policy multiple times over the years, and the version currently circulating online is outdated.

title: Liquor, Meat Offerings, Midnight Rituals: Mumbai's Gym Trainer-Turned Godman
content: Rhythm Panchal operated out of a vacant plot in Malad and held a darbar (court) every Thursday and Saturday from 8 pm to 7 am.

title: Can Iran Legally Collect Tolls On Vessels Passing Strait Of Hormuz?
content: UN Convention on the Law of the Sea provides vessels a right of unimpeded "transit passage" through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: How A US Blockade On Iran Is Forcing Sanctioned Ships To Turn Back
content: Iran-linked vessels appear to have jammed or faked their locations in some instances, complicating an uncertain and risky shipping situation.

title: "38 Fake Cases Against Me": Congress Flags Madhya Pradesh Minister's Remark
content: The remarks have triggered a political row, with the Congress alleging that the minister concealed the information about pending criminal cases in his election affidavit.

title: 'Politicisation by Pakistan': Lalit Modi blames PCB for India-Pak cricket freeze
content: Former IPL chairman Lalit Modi expressed pessimism about India and Pakistan resuming bilateral cricket soon, citing significant political interference within the Pakistan Cricket Board as the primary obstacle. He believes external pressures have politicized decisions, hindering any possibility of a series, even at neutral venues, and fears the situation will worsen.

title: 'My guarantee': PM Modi says no state to face injustice due to delimitation
content: The Congress on Thursday alleged that the government wants to "bulldoze" delimitation in the name of women's reservation, and asserted that if it is really committed to implementing the women's quota law it should do so immediately on the basis of the current strength of Lok Sabha. The opposition party also alleged that the BJP was using delimitation as a "political weapon" to increase its influence.

title: How change in H-1B selection system has been a boon to employees already in the US
content: US businesses are witnessing significantly higher H-1B visa selection rates, with some reporting over 50% success. This surge follows the Department of Homeland Security's shift from a random lottery to a wage-weighted system, prioritizing higher salary offers. The new rule, effective February 27, 2026, aims to reshape the selection process for skilled foreign workers.

title: LeT co-founder Amir Hamza shot at in Lahore in second attack within a year
content: Lashkar-e-Taiba co-founder Amir Hamza was shot by unknown gunmen in Lahore, marking a second assassination attempt in less than a year. The 67-year-old, a designated global terrorist, was attacked outside a TV station while accompanied by a Pakistani judge. This incident follows a previous attempt where he survived a shooting last May.

title: BSF officer killed in fuel depot fire at Bengal border outpost
content: Tragedy struck the Bankra border outpost in West Bengal's North 24 Parganas as a devastating fire engulfed a fuel depot. Assistant Sub Inspector Jagi Ram tragically lost his life after being trapped inside during a blast that ignited the blaze. The depot supplied fuel for BSF boats patrolling the Sundarbans. Authorities are investigating the incident.

title: 'Don't want credit': Top quotes from PM Modi's Lok Sabha speech on women's reservation bill
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi hailed the women's reservation Bill as a defining moment for India's democracy, emphasizing the need for greater female representation in policymaking. He asserted that the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, providing 33% reservation for women, will give India a new direction, linking it to the nation's ancient democratic roots and the vision of a 'Viksit Bharat'.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection day 29: Ranveer Singh film marches past Rs 1,100 cr
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has officially breached the Rs. 1,100 crore net milestone in India on Day 29. From its massive Rs. 90 crore opening day to becoming the first Hindi film to enter the Rs. 1,000 crore club, Aditya Dhar’s spy thriller starring Ranveer Singh is rewriting history. Read on to learn more about the latest live tracking updates, day-wise collection breakdown for Week 1, 2, and 3 and a recap of the worldwide gross hitting Rs. 1,733 crore despite the IPL 2026 craze.

title: Newborns left in dustbins, buses, forests: MP tops infant abandonment for 5th straight year
content: Madhya Pradesh leads the nation in infant abandonment cases. Shocking incidents reveal newborns left in dangerous situations, some surviving miraculously. The National Crime Records Bureau highlights this ongoing crisis. Fear, social stigma, and policy pressures drive these extreme acts. Addressing this requires urgent awareness and support systems.

title: 'Dosa, idly' row: From Jitesh's clip to DJ — what sparked CSK vs RCB controversy
content: In the complaint, CSK said the playing of the track "Dosa, idli, sambar, chutney, chutney" during the match was "not in good taste." The song is often used in memes linked to South Indian stereotypes.

title: 'World’s largest water fight': 191 killed in 3 days of Thailand’s Songkran festival
content: Thailand's Songkran festival, known for its water fights, has seen a tragic start with at least 191 road accident fatalities in the first three days. Speeding and drunk driving were the primary causes, despite government safety efforts. This surge highlights the ongoing road safety challenges during the "seven dangerous days" of the festival.

title: MI vs PBKS: Shreyas Iyer to Jasprit Bumrah - 5 key player battles to watch
content: Mumbai Indians face Punjab Kings in a crucial IPL 2026 clash at Wankhede Stadium. MI, struggling with three consecutive losses and a doubtful Rohit Sharma, host an unbeaten PBKS. This historic rivalry is evenly matched, with key individual battles set to decide the outcome.

title: New route? How are Iran-linked ships passing through Strait of Hormuz despite US blockade
content: Amidst a US naval blockade, Iran-linked vessels navigated the Strait of Hormuz, signalling potential route shifts.  Traffic remains significantly reduced, with heightened checks by both US and Iranian forces slowing crossings. Conflicting claims emerge regarding successful breaches, as electronic interference and disabled tracking systems obscure full visibility in this critical global oil transit zone.

title: 'Religion-based reservation unconstitutional': Amit Shah & Akhilesh Yadav clash over delimitation
content: Home Minister Amit Shah and SP chief Akhilesh Yadav clashed in Parliament over the Women's Reservation Bill. Yadav questioned the urgency without an updated Census, demanding a caste-based enumeration. Shah assured that a caste census would be conducted after the ongoing Census process, while also stating religion-based reservations are unconstitutional.

title: Amid TCS Nashik row, Tech Mahindra denies religious bias allegations at Mumbai’s Goregaon office
content: Tech Mahindra denies allegations of religious bias at its Goregaon office after social media claims surfaced. This follows heightened scrutiny on IT firms, with TCS Nashik facing a police probe over sexual harassment and religious conversion allegations. Nine FIRs have been registered at TCS Nashik, leading to several arrests.

title: Parents in Portugal, 5-year-old tied to iron gate by cop grandmother; police dept acts
content: An assistant sub-inspector in Faridkot was suspended after allegedly tying her five-year-old granddaughter to a gate. Neighbors intervened after hearing the child wailing and found her hands and feet bound. The ASI, who was caring for the child while her parents were abroad, was suspended after a video of the incident went viral.

title: 'Serial bride & fake dad' flee with grooms' cash, gold near Mumbai
content: Kashimira police apprehended a man and woman for a wedding scam, where the bride would abscond with valuables days after the ceremony.  The duo, targeting divorced men over 40, were involved in five Maharashtra cases.  The man posed as family to arrange marriages, with the woman then fleeing with cash and jewellery.  They have been remanded in custody.

title: Hormuz disruptions hit! LPG imports halve from Feb levels, domestic output also impacted
content: Ongoing disruptions to energy flows from the Gulf linked to the US-Iran war continue to unsettle global oil and gas markets, and a ceasefire in place for about a week has done little to improve the availability of cooking gas.

title: Noida workers protest: UP govt hikes minimum wages, but can exporters afford it?
content: Garment exporters in Uttar Pradesh face a dilemma as a recent wage hike for workers clashes with rising input costs and squeezed margins.  Ongoing labor unrest in Noida factories is causing concern among global buyers, who are seeking clarity on potential shipment delays and may shift orders to competing nations.

title: HAL’s Light Utility Helicopter: Specs, altitude edge and why it matters for India’s Siachen ops
content: In the super high altitude border areas in both the North and the North East, the Cheetah helicopter is not just a machine, its a lifeline for the troops deployed on desolate posts in defence of India. In these areas the helicopter is a lifeline and often the sole mode of transport. As the Chetak and the Cheetah helicopter head towards retirement, a new helicopter India's own Light Utility Helicopter is poised to take their place.

title: 'Saari nazar utaar denge': PSL CEO wards off 'evil eye' for Babar Azam - Watch
content: The clip surfaced after Zalmi’s match in the ongoing 11th edition of the Pakistan Super League and showed a light exchange during a public interaction. "Aaj saari nazar utaar denge," a person is heard saying in the video.

title: Dismissed UP cop kills beggar to fake his own death, burns body, leaves ID near remains
content: A former Uttar Pradesh Police constable has been arrested in Hathras for allegedly murdering a beggar. The accused then burned the beggar's body and placed his own belongings nearby to fake his death. Police uncovered the elaborate plot during their investigation. The dismissed constable, Ramveer Singh, has a history of serious criminal charges across multiple districts.

title: Women’s Reservation Bill: How numbers stack up in Lok Sabha and what delimitation could change
content: Parliament is set to debate the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, aiming for 33% women's reservation in legislatures. This constitutional amendment, passed in 2023, will be implemented after delimitation based on the 2027 Census. While the government champions women's empowerment, opposition raises concerns over the delimitation process and its potential impact on state representation, creating a north-south divide.

title: How this teenager survived a plane crash - injured, alone and in harsh land
content: At 17, Juliane Koepcke survived a plane crash, falling 3,000 meters into the Amazon rainforest. She then spent 11 days navigating the jungle, a place she knew from her childhood, before being found by fishermen. Her survival, marked by resilience and familiarity with her surroundings, remains a powerful testament to endurance.

title: New record! Apple’s India vendors export $2.5 bn components to China; may reach $3.5 bn
content: Several Apple suppliers in India contributed to shipments to China, including Foxconn, Tata Electronics, Tata-owned Pegatron Technology India, Motherson, Salcomp, TRIL Bangalore and Yuzhan Technology.

title: 'Trying to get a little breathing room': Trump says Israel-Lebanon talks to be on Thursday
content: President Trump announced that Israel and Lebanon are set to hold peace talks, aiming to ease long-standing tensions. This follows a historic trilateral meeting hosted by the US, where both nations expressed willingness for direct negotiations, a ceasefire, and a broader peace framework.

title: Touching moment: Kohli stunned as LSG youngster tries to touch his feet - Watch
content: During an interaction after the match, Tiwari bent down to touch Kohli's feet, a gesture used to show respect. Kohli appeared taken aback and responded with a smile. The video has gone viral on social media.

title: CBSE Class 10 result 2026: How did Delhi perform in this year’s board examinations?
content: CBSE has declared the Class 10 results 2026 with a 93.70% pass rate, showing a slight rise over last year and stable national performance. Delhi outperformed the average with 97.39%, led by strong results in East and West regions. Low compartment rates and high participation underline consistent outcomes despite ongoing exam reforms.

title: Another injury blow for CSK, Indian international ruled out of IPL 2026
content: Chennai Super Kings pacer Khaleel Ahmed has been ruled out of IPL 2026 due to a quadriceps injury, dealing a blow to the franchise’s bowling attack.

title: CSK knock BCCI's door, lodge complaint against RCB for 'Dosa idli' song
content: Chennai Super Kings have lodged a complaint with the BCCI over alleged misconduct during their IPL 2026 clash. The complaint cites inappropriate in-stadium entertainment, including a song that CSK felt stereotyped their identity, and remarks made by the stadium DJ after player dismissals. This adds to the growing rivalry between the two franchises.

title: Shatrughan Sinha tells Gulshan Grover, ‘you should’ve played Akshaye Khanna's role in Dhurandhar’
content: Gulshan Grover, who is currently promoting his upcoming series Matka King, believes that cinema is witnessing a full-circle moment—with trends, characters and storytelling styles from the past making a strong comeback. The actor also shared a recent anecdote from a dinner with industry veterans, including Shatrughan Sinha, Rakesh Roshan and Shashi Ranjan. He further revealed how Sinha even imagined him in Akshaye Khanna's Rehman Dakait's role in Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar.

title: TCS Nashik office staff told to work from home amid religious conversion, harassment claims
content: TCS has suspended operations at its Nashik BPO center following an escalated probe into alleged sexual harassment and forced religious conversion. Police are investigating an organized pattern of behavior among accused staff, including HR officials, who allegedly targeted financially vulnerable employees. Separately, Infosys issued a statement addressing similar allegations at its Pune facility.

title: Price prediction: Will gold head to Rs 1.65 lakh/10g & silver rise to Rs 2.80 lakh/kg?
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: Gold and silver prices may extend gains in the coming days, says Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: In patient mix-up, BHU doctors operate on 71-year-old’s hip instead of spine, woman dies
content: A 71-year-old woman, Radhika Devi, underwent a partial surgery for spinal tumour due to patient misidentification at BHU trauma centre. She was mistakenly operated on for hip replacement. After further complications and multiple surgeries, she passed away. An investigation is underway into the gross medical negligence.

title: Comedy of errors in PSL! Chaos unfolds as run-out chance goes begging - Watch
content: The incident took place in the final over of Quetta’s innings. Ali Raza bowled a waist-high no-ball to Usman Tariq on the fourth delivery. On the next ball, which was a free hit, Abrar Ahmed was on strike.
